milestones.title: Foundation & Core Engine
milestones.vision: Build the complete monitoring engine — from ping ingestion to miss detection to alerting — with a functional dashboard. After M001, a developer can create a monitor, ping it from a cron job, and get alerted when the job fails.
success_criteria: Developer can sign up via Clerk and access a protected dashboard
Developer can create a monitor and receive a unique ping URL
Pinging the URL records the heartbeat with timestamp and metadata
Miss detection engine correctly identifies overdue monitors based on cron schedule + grace period
Email alert fires within 2 minutes of a missed ping
Slack and Discord webhook alerts can be configured and fire correctly
Dashboard shows real-time monitor status (up/down/new) and ping history
Cron expressions are parsed and displayed in human-readable format

key_risks.whyItMatters: Cron expression parsing + grace periods + timezone handling is the hardest technical problem. If this doesn't work reliably, the product has no value.
Serverless Postgres may have latency on ping ingestion. Ping endpoint must respond fast (<200ms) to not break customer cron jobs.
The miss detection loop runs on an interval. If Railway's process management drops the scheduler, missed alerts go undetected.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Cron parser correctly computes next expected ping, grace period window works, and miss detection fires alerts for overdue monitors
Ping endpoint responds under 200ms including DB write on Neon free tier
Scheduler loop runs continuously and recovers from transient failures

milestones.verification_contract: Vitest unit tests for cron parsing, monitor CRUD, miss detection logic. Integration tests for ping endpoint and alert dispatch.
milestones.verification_integration: Real Neon DB connection for ping writes, real Resend API for email alerts, real Slack/Discord webhook delivery.
milestones.verification_operational: Express server starts cleanly, scheduler loop runs and recovers, health endpoint responds.
milestones.verification_uat: Manual walkthrough: create monitor → copy ping URL → curl it → wait for schedule → verify alert fires on miss.
definition_of_done: All slice deliverables are complete and verified
Ping endpoint accepts GET/POST and records heartbeats under 200ms
Miss detection correctly identifies overdue monitors and triggers alerts
Email, Slack, and Discord alerts deliver successfully
Dashboard shows monitor CRUD, live status, and ping history
Auth protects all routes with tenant isolation
All tests pass, ESLint clean, tsc --noEmit clean
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: R001, R002, R003, R004, R006, R008\nPartially covers: R009 (infrastructure cost validated locally, not in prod)\nLeaves for later: R005 (billing), R007 (landing page), R010 (free tier enforcement)
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 → S02

Produces:
- Drizzle schema and query helpers for monitors, pings, alerts
- Monitor CRUD API endpoints (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/monitors)
- Ping ingestion endpoint (POST/GET /api/ping/:slug)
- Clerk auth middleware attached to request context
- Monitor model with schedule, gracePeriod, timezone, status fields

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- All API endpoints that the dashboard will consume
- Clerk auth session for frontend integration
- Shared types from @cronguard/shared

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- Alert configuration endpoints (CRUD alert channels per monitor)
- Monitor status computation (up/down/new based on miss detection)
- Alert history endpoint for dashboard display

# S01: DB Schema, Ping Endpoint & Auth

**Full API foundation: 6-table Drizzle schema, Clerk auth with tenant isolation, monitor CRUD with plan-tier enforcement, and ping ingestion with <200ms response guarantee**

## What Happened

S01 delivered the complete API backend for CronGuard in 5 tasks across a single feature branch, squash-merged as PR #1.

T01 established the data layer: 6-table Drizzle schema (users, monitors, pings, alert_channels, monitor_alert_channels, alerts) with indexes optimized for miss detection queries, Neon WebSocket pool client, and migration infrastructure. Dockerfile included from day 1 for Railway deployment.

T02 added Clerk JWT verification middleware (requireAuth) for tenant isolation via userId. Webhook endpoint with Svix signature verification handles user lifecycle events. 6 unit tests cover the auth middleware.

T03 created shared Zod schemas in packages/shared for monitor and ping validation, importable by both API and future frontend.

T04 implemented full monitor CRUD (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) with plan-tier enforcement (free: 5, pro: 50, team: 200). All routes tenant-isolated via userId from auth middleware.

T05 delivered the ping ingestion endpoint (GET/POST /api/ping/:slug) — public, no auth. Responds before DB write for <200ms guarantee. LRU cache (1000 entries, 60s TTL) for slug lookups. Dual rate limiting (120/min per IP, 10/min per slug). State machine transitions on ping receipt.

## Verification

Full workspace verification: TypeScript 0 errors, ESLint 0 issues, 6/6 tests passing. PR #1 code-reviewed (security, performance, type safety) and squash-merged to main.

## Requirements Advanced

None.

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Operational Readiness

None.

## Deviations

None.

## Known Limitations

None.

## Follow-ups

Extract computeNextExpected (duplicated in monitors.ts and ping.ts) into shared utility when touching either file. Broaden test coverage beyond auth middleware. LRU cache not invalidated on monitor updates (60s TTL is acceptable for now).

## Files Created/Modified

- `apps/api/src/db/schema.ts` — 6-table Drizzle schema with indexes
- `apps/api/src/db/client.ts` — Neon WebSocket pool client
- `apps/api/src/middleware/auth.ts` — Clerk JWT verification middleware
- `apps/api/src/routes/monitors.ts` — Monitor CRUD with plan-tier enforcement
- `apps/api/src/routes/ping.ts` — Ping ingestion with LRU cache and rate limiting
- `apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts` — Clerk webhook user sync via Svix
- `packages/shared/src/schemas/monitor.ts` — Zod schemas for monitor create/update
- `packages/shared/src/schemas/ping.ts` — Zod schemas for ping ingestion
slices.full_uat_md:
# S01: DB Schema, Ping Endpoint & Auth — UAT

**Milestone:** M001
**Written:** 2026-05-08T13:16:37.076Z

## UAT: S01 — DB Schema, Ping Endpoint & Auth

### Prerequisites
- Neon database provisioned with DATABASE_URL set
- Clerk application configured with CLERK_SECRET_KEY and CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY
- `npm install` completed at root

### Test Cases

#### 1. Health Check
```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/health
# Expected: { "status": "ok" }
```

#### 2. Create Monitor (Authenticated)
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/monitors \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <clerk_jwt>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name": "Nightly Backup", "schedule": "0 2 * * *", "gracePeriodMinutes": 10}'
# Expected: 201 with monitor object including slug (nanoid 21)
```

#### 3. List Monitors (Tenant Isolated)
```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/api/monitors \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <clerk_jwt>"
# Expected: 200 with array of only the authenticated user's monitors
```

#### 4. Ping a Monitor (No Auth)
```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/api/ping/<slug>
# Expected: { "ok": true } with <200ms response
```

#### 5. Rate Limiting
```bash
# Send 11 requests to same slug within 1 minute
for i in $(seq 1 11); do curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000/api/ping/<slug>; done
# Expected: 10th returns 200, 11th returns 429
```

#### 6. Plan-Tier Enforcement
```bash
# On free tier, try creating 6th monitor
# Expected: 403 with error about monitor limit
```

#### 7. Unauthorized Access
```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/api/monitors
# Expected: 401 Unauthorized
```
slices.goal: Establish the data layer, auth boundary, and ping ingestion — the foundation everything else builds on.
slices.sketch_scope: Drizzle schema for users/monitors/pings/alerts, Clerk auth middleware, monitor CRUD endpoints, ping ingestion endpoint with sub-200ms response time. Proves Neon free tier latency is acceptable.

slices.title: Miss Detection Engine & Alert Dispatch
slices.demo: After this: a monitor that hasn't pinged within its schedule + grace period triggers an email alert via Resend and optionally Slack/Discord webhooks.
slices.goal: Miss detection engine that transitions monitors through grace→down states, dispatches alerts via email (Resend) and webhooks (Slack/Discord/generic), and sends recovery notifications when monitors come back up.
slices.success_criteria: Monitor missing its schedule transitions through grace→down. Email alert fires on down. Slack/Discord/webhook alerts fire on down. Recovery alert fires when monitor pings after being down. All alerts are idempotent via missKey. computeNextExpected extracted to shared utility.
slices.sketch_scope: Cron expression parsing with cron-parser, next-expected-ping computation, grace period logic, scheduler loop (setInterval or cron-based), alert dispatch to Resend email + Slack webhook + Discord webhook. Alert preferences per monitor.

slices.title: Dashboard UI
slices.demo: After this: developer can sign in, see all monitors with live status indicators, create/edit/delete monitors, view ping history timeline, and configure alert channels — all in a polished Tailwind UI.
slices.goal: Build the React dashboard where developers manage monitors, view status, and configure alerts.
slices.sketch_scope: TanStack Router for app shell and routes, TanStack Query for data fetching, TanStack Form for monitor create/edit. Monitor list with status badges, monitor detail with ping history, alert channel configuration. Clerk auth UI integration.

# T05: Ping ingestion endpoint with respond-before-write, LRU cache, and dual rate limiting

**Ping ingestion endpoint with respond-before-write, LRU cache, and dual rate limiting**

## What Happened

Implemented GET/POST /api/ping/:slug — public, no auth required. Responds immediately before DB write via setImmediate for <200ms guarantee. LRU cache (1000 entries, 60s TTL) for slug→monitor lookups. Dual rate limiting: 120/min per IP, 10/min per slug with draft-7 headers. State transitions (new/down/grace → up) with alertSentAt clearing on recovery. Mounted before Clerk middleware in Express stack for minimum overhead.

tasks.title: Miss detection scheduler
tasks.description: setInterval polling at 30s. Queries monitors past nextExpectedPingAt + gracePeriod. State transitions: up→grace (past expected), grace→down (past grace). FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. Advances nextExpectedPingAt for stale down monitors.
tasks.verify: tsc --noEmit && npx eslint . && unit tests for state transition logic

tasks.title: Alert dispatch (Resend, Slack, Discord, webhook)
tasks.description: Resend SDK for email alerts. HTTP POST for Slack/Discord/generic webhooks. Promise.allSettled for concurrent dispatch. 3-attempt retry with exponential backoff. Idempotent via missKey unique constraint. Alert templates for miss and recovery.
tasks.verify: tsc --noEmit && npx eslint . && unit tests for dispatch logic
inputs: alerts table with missKey unique constraint

tasks.title: Recovery alerts + integration tests
tasks.description: Wire recovery alerts into ping handler (down→up transition dispatches through configured channels). Integration tests covering miss detection → alert creation → dispatch. Structured logging for scheduler observability.
tasks.verify: tsc --noEmit && npx eslint . && full test suite passes
expected_output: Recovery alert dispatch on down→up transition in ping handler
Integration tests for full miss→alert→recovery pipeline

decisions.choice: Vite SPA + separate Express API (no Next.js)
decisions.rationale: Owner's expertise is React + Vite + TanStack ecosystem. Separate API enables independent scaling and Railway deployment. No SSR needed for dashboard app.

decisions.choice: apps/web, apps/api, packages/shared with npm workspaces
decisions.rationale: Shared types and utilities between frontend and backend. Single repo simplifies CI and deployment. npm workspaces over Turborepo to keep tooling minimal.
Generous free tier (0.5GB storage, 190 compute hours/mo), serverless Postgres with branching, familiar SQL. Keeps costs at $0 until significant scale.

decisions.choice: Free (5 monitors, email), Pro $9/mo (50 monitors, Slack/Discord/webhooks, 30d retention), Team $29/mo (200 monitors, SMS/PagerDuty, 90d retention)
decisions.rationale: $9 sits below psychological $10 threshold, undercuts Healthchecks ($20) and Cronitor ($20+). Free tier at 5 monitors is more generous than competitors (1-3). 112 Pro customers = $1K MRR target.
Free up to 10K MAU, drop-in React components, JWT verification for API routes, webhook events for user lifecycle. Owner has existing experience.
Free tier (100 emails/day, 3K/mo), simple API, React Email for templates. Sufficient for MVP alert volume.

decisions.choice: Single setInterval polling loop at 30s granularity, DB-stored next_expected_at, stateless and restartable
decisions.rationale: Simplest correct solution. DB is the single source of truth — no in-memory state to lose on restart. 30s interval catches 1-minute grace periods. FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED enables future multi-process scaling. Single process on Railway handles 10K+ monitors.

decisions.choice: 4-state machine: new → up → grace → down → up. Grace period transitions are silent; alerts fire only on down and recovery.
decisions.rationale: Grace state prevents false alerts for late-but-acceptable pings. alert_sent_at column provides atomic deduplication. Advancing next_expected_at for stale down monitors prevents alert storms on restart.

decisions.choice: Respond before DB write (fire-and-forget). LRU cache for monitor slug lookup. In-memory write buffer with 3-attempt retry for DB failures.
decisions.rationale: Guarantees <200ms response regardless of Neon cold start latency. Monitor lookup from LRU cache is <1ms. Losing a ping write on DB failure is acceptable — the monitor would show as missed anyway. Industry standard (Healthchecks, Cronitor).
Compact for curl one-liners, unguessable (brute force impractical even at 1000 req/s). UUID v4 is too long for CLI ergonomics. Slug serves as bearer token — no auth required on ping endpoint.

decisions.choice: User-level alert_channels table with JSONB config + monitor_alert_channels join table. Channels are reusable across monitors.
decisions.rationale: A user configures their Slack webhook once, then assigns it to multiple monitors. JSONB config handles heterogeneous channel types without per-type columns. miss_key unique index on alerts table provides idempotent deduplication.

decisions.choice: TanStack Router with pathless _auth layout guard, TanStack Query polling (60s list, 30s detail), dark-first theme, shadcn/ui (canary) for primitives
decisions.rationale: Polling matches cron job data cadence exactly — no WebSocket complexity for MVP. Pathless auth layout is the canonical TanStack Router pattern for protected routes. shadcn/ui gives accessible Radix primitives with Tailwind v4 support, owned in-repo. Dark-first because developer/devops user base.

decisions.choice: Neon serverless WebSocket pool for API routes, Neon HTTP driver as fallback option for ping path
decisions.rationale: WebSocket pool keeps connections warm across requests, dropping latency from 300-500ms (cold) to 20-60ms (warm). HTTP driver is stateless and faster from cold start but ping endpoint responds before DB write anyway, making driver choice less critical for that path.

decisions.decision: Deployment platform for all CronGuard services
decisions.choice: Railway (Hobby plan) for compute + Neon Postgres (free tier) for database. Estimated ~$9-13/mo total. Dockerfile included from day 1 for portability to Hetzner + Coolify when revenue justifies it.
decisions.rationale: Railway wins on time-to-ship for a solo weekend build: git-push deploys, first-class monorepo support (watch paths), managed SSL, zero DevOps. Render eliminated ($32-51/mo over budget). Fly.io eliminated (no free tier, $38/mo managed Postgres, Docker-only). Hetzner+Coolify is cheaper (~$8-11/mo) but 1-3hr setup + ongoing maintenance costs time that should go to product. Railway at $8-12/mo compute (Neon handles Postgres at $0) is within budget — 2 paying customers cover infrastructure. Migration path: add docker-compose.yml from day 1, move to Hetzner when bill hits $40-50/mo.
decisions.revisable: Yes — migrate to Hetzner + Coolify when Railway bill exceeds $40-50/mo (signals sufficient revenue to justify DevOps time)
